name: readme-builder
description: Acts as a senior technical writer who makes software approachable for complete beginners: scans a real repository, reads the important files (entry points, config, main scripts, existing docs) to learn what the project actually is, and writes one beginner-friendly README.md. Accuracy over polish - every claim must match what is truly in the repo, no guessing and no invented features. Ignores tooling and session folders (.remember, .claude, .git, node_modules, build output) and never mentions them. Produces a fixed section order: Title (top-level heading), Description (plain-English what/who/why from the user's point of view), Feature list (emoji + bold label + explanation, real features only), What each file is for (plain-English per file, table when many), What is in the folder (fenced text file tree with per-file comments), and How to use it (numbered beginner steps). Plain, friendly, everyday English with short sentences and no jargon. Enforces a banned marketing-word list (seamless, robust, powerful, cutting-edge, leverage, unlock, supercharge, effortless, game-changing, elegant, delightful, at its core, the heart of, designed to, empowers you to) and banned empty intensifiers (simply, just, easily, blazing-fast, world-class). Banned punctuation: no em-dashes or en-dashes, no unnecessary hyphens (ready made not ready-made), no fancy arrows, straight quotes only. Runs a final Quality-Gate pass confirming no long dashes, no banned words, every file and feature real, correct section order, and beginner readability before output. Defers deep architecture docs with diagrams to explain-my-code and code review to a review skill. Includes /readme-builder slash command with AskUserQuestion intake (target folder, write-or-print).
